reforms.description: The Teknogirişim (TechnoEntrepreneurship) programme was proposed by the Ministry of Science, Industry and Technology to support the development of technology-based startups and enhance entrepreneurship in Turkey. Additionally, tax incentives for business angels and venture capitalists have been introduced to facilitate financing for innovative investments in small firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to stimulate entrepreneurship and innovation, which are critical for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax incentives and the Teknogirişim programme are currently in effect, supporting startups in Turkey.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of significant tax incentives and a dedicated programme for startups represents a structural change in the support for the entrepreneurial ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: To facilitate the financing of innovative investments in small firms, tax incentives have been offered to business angels and venture capitalists.

reforms.description: KOSGEB administers eight support schemes aimed at enhancing the capabilities of SMEs, including the R&D and Innovation Support Programme, which co-funds projects to improve innovation and productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By co-funding projects, the programme enhances the innovation capacity of SMEs, which is vital for economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The support schemes are actively administered by KOSGEB and are available to SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of multiple support schemes represents a significant enhancement of the innovation support framework for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: Eight support schemes are currently administered by KOSGEB, with a total annual budget of TRY 280 million.

reforms.description: A new scheme jointly introduced by Iskur and the Union of Chambers (TOBB) will subsidise the costs of re-training for the unemployed, aiming to make training more relevant to the labour market and encourage re-hiring by employers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the skills of the unemployed, the scheme aims to improve employability and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme is currently operational and providing subsidies for re-training.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a targeted re-training scheme represents a significant step towards aligning workforce skills with market needs.
reforms.source_quote: A recent scheme jointly introduced by Iskur and the Union of Chambers (TOBB) is a promising innovation.
